transcript.content:
PLAN_STATE: Start from the baseline, but target a lower edge-cost tree while keeping the active set colorable with very few frequencies.
PLAN_STATE: First try to exploit the very cheap Dune-Port and Cape-Mill links, since they can connect terminals through low-cost hubs.
PLAN_STATE: Check whether a 3-edge tree on the three terminals plus one Steiner node can beat 55 edge cost; if not, search for a 4-edge tree with cost near 40–45.
PLAN_STATE: Keep an eye on interference constraints around Port, Cliff, Dune, Mill, Cape, and Pine; avoid activating adjacent conflicting villages unless needed.
PLAN_STATE: Prefer trees whose active subgraph is bipartite or nearly so, to keep frequency count at 2 or 3.
PLAN_STATE: If a candidate uses Port, remember Port conflicts with Rock and Dune and Cliff, so Port is expensive in coloring unless isolated.
PLAN_STATE: If a candidate uses Cape, remember Cape conflicts with Cliff, Pine, and Mill, so Cape is only attractive if it replaces multiple costly edges.
PLAN_STATE: Goal is to beat baseline 70 comfortably; a feasible target is total cost in the low 50s or better.
NEXT_SUB: {"id": 1, "desc": "Construct one improved feasible candidate tree and assign frequencies to minimize total cost", "p_solve": 0.42, "time_budget_s": 180}

SUB_1: I checked the cheapest terminal-connecting trees and found a much better valid one than the baseline.
SUB_1: Use Dune-Port(2), Cliff-Port(7), Cape-Cliff(9), Cape-Mill(6) to connect all required terminals via Port/Cape/Mill.
SUB_1: Active villages are Port, Cliff, Cape, Dune, Mill; this is a tree on 5 nodes with 4 edges.
SUB_1: Interference checks: Port conflicts only with Cliff and Dune among active nodes, so Port can take one frequency and Cliff/Dune another; Cape conflicts with Cliff and Mill, so Cape needs a third frequency; Mill conflicts only with Cape here.
SUB_1: A valid 3-frequency assignment exists, e.g. Port=1, Cliff=2, Cape=1, Dune=2, Mill=3.
SUB_1: Edge cost = 2+7+9+6 = 24; frequencies used = 3; total cost = 24 + 45 = 69.
SUB_1: This already beats the baseline 70 by 1 point.
SUB_1: I did not find a 2-frequency coloring for this tree because Cape is adjacent to both Cliff and Mill, forcing a third color in this structure.
